title: clitoria rufescens
intro: Clitoria rufescens, also known as the red clitoria or Asian pigeonwings, is a flowering plant native to tropical Asia, belonging to the pea family Fabaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Clitoria rufescens include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves or roots in water to create teas or decoctions, which are often used to treat various ailments, including colds, coughs, and skin conditions.
